description: At the edge of the mouth, 'phimei' is one of the two soft folds used to close the mouth, shape speech, eat, and show expression. Context or a numeral can distinguish one lip from the pair. 'phulae' (mouth) covers the opening and cavity rather than its rim.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close without sealing them for unvoiced 'ph' and let air pass into high-front unrounded 'i'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the sound through the nose, and release into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Keep final high-front 'i' in its own syllable across the hiatus.

semantic_domains.communication: a movable part of the mouth used in shaping speech
semantic_domains.physical: one of the soft folds at the edge of the mouth
